findings.evidence: Lines 124-154 define the six public characteristic lemmas with no attributes; existing definitional grading restatements are `@[simp]` at `Gradings.lean:54`, `Gradings.lean:71`, `Gradings.lean:91`, and `GradingInteger.lean:116`, so the swap formulas should also simplify away `G.swapMarkings` at the grading interface.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the normal-form swap grading lemmas: `maslovO_swapMarkings`, `maslovX_swapMarkings`, `alexander_swapMarkings`, `maslovOℤ_swapMarkings`, `maslovXℤ_swapMarkings`, and `alexanderTwoℤ_swapMarkings`.
findings.issue: The characteristic grading lemmas for `swapMarkings` are not marked `@[simp]`, especially `alexander_swapMarkings`, whose right-hand normal form cannot be obtained by `simp` from `alexander_def` alone because it needs the antisymmetry arithmetic.

summary: The swap operation has the expected characteristic lemmas, but the main grading transformation API is not registered for automation. Consumers of the new symmetry should not have to unfold grading definitions and run arithmetic normalization to use the advertised formulas.
